statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, sort it in-place using bubble sort algorithm. The function should return true if the array is sorted and false otherwise.

examples.explanation: The array is sorted in ascending order.
The array is not sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Check the swap logic in the solution.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: bool Solution::solve(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.size() - 1; i++) { if (nums[i] > nums[i + 1]) { swap(nums[i + 1], nums[i + 2]); } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean solve(int[] nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.length - 1; i++) { if (nums[i] > nums[i + 1]) { int temp = nums[i + 1]; nums[i + 1] = nums[i + 2]; nums[i + 2] = temp; } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.python: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: for i in range(len(nums) - 1): if nums[i] > nums[i + 1]: nums[i + 1], nums[i + 2] = nums[i + 2], nums[i + 1]; return True;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { for (let i = 0; i < nums.length - 1; i++) { if (nums[i] > nums[i + 1]) { let temp = nums[i + 1]; nums[i + 1] = nums[i + 2]; nums[i + 2] = temp; } } return true; }

solutions.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: for i in range(len(nums) - 1): if nums[i] > nums[i + 1]: nums[i], nums[i + 1] = nums[i + 1], nums[i]; return sorted(nums) == nums;
solutions.cpp: #include <bits/stdc++.h> int main() { Solution s; vector<int> nums = {4, 2, 3, 1}; bool res = s.solve(nums); cout << (res ? "true" : "false") << endl; return 0; }
